mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of UNRATE < 4.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or UNRATE fails to decline by >0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market dynamics, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not positive when PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5.0% simultaneously or the interaction term's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when INDPRO increases by >1.5% over 3 months or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) pressures central banks to accelerate rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not negative when FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and CPIAUCSL rises by >0.5pp over 1 month or the interaction term's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicate rising consumer demand for durable goods, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when HOUST increases by >10k over 1 month or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer spending (RSAFS) above $450 billion signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not positive when RSAFS exceeds 450k or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad-based economic expansion, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through macroeconomic confidence effects.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not positive when GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise by >1% over 6 months or the interaction term's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).
